title: Sub-lemma 3: rational-to-real transfer via monotone coupling at lower threshold + continuity from above

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Most compressed step in the write-up — must be fully expanded.** For irrational $p\in[0,1/c)$, pick rationals $p_i\uparrow p$ with $p_i<p$. Prove $\Pr_{v\sim\mathrm{Bern}(p)^m}[\langle w,v\rangle\ge p]\ge p$ in three explicit steps:

1. **Rational result at the lower threshold.** By the matrix-coupling argument (Sub-lemmas 1, 2a–2c), $\Pr_{u\sim\mathrm{Bern}(p_i)^m}[\langle w,u\rangle\ge p_i]\ge p_i$. Note the *underlying law is Bernoulli$(p_i)$* and the *threshold is $p_i$*.
2. **Monotone coupling.** Couple $v\sim\mathrm{Bern}(p)^m$ and $v_i\sim\mathrm{Bern}(p_i)^m$ on one space with $v\ge v_i$ coordinatewise (e.g. $v_j=\mathbf 1[U_j\le p]$, $(v_i)_j=\mathbf1[U_j\le p_i]$ for i.i.d. uniforms $U_j$). Since $w>0$, $\langle w,v\rangle\ge\langle w,v_i\rangle$, hence
$$\Pr_{v\sim\mathrm{Bern}(p)}[\langle w,v\rangle\ge p_i]\ \ge\ \Pr[\langle w,v_i\rangle\ge p_i]\ \ge\ p_i.$$
   The threshold must be $p_i$ here (coupling at threshold $p$ would not work).
3. **Continuity from above.** The events $A_i=\{\langle w,v\rangle\ge p_i\}$ are *decreasing* as $p_i\uparrow p$ and $\bigcap_i A_i=\{\langle w,v\rangle\ge p\}$ (since $X\ge p_i\ \forall i\iff X\ge\sup_i p_i=p$). By continuity of measure from above,
$$\Pr[\langle w,v\rangle\ge p]=\lim_i\Pr[A_i]\ge\lim_i p_i=p.$$

Also dispose of the trivial boundary $p=0$ ($\Pr[\cdot\ge0]=1\ge0$). Each of the three steps and the intersection identity must be stated explicitly.
